learnings.text: The existing config.py imports from .config in models.py work correctly - the Integrations/recall_ai/ directory is properly structured as a Python package

SQLite models successfully created with comprehensive test suite. Key implementation details: use try/except for imports to handle both relative (.config) and absolute (config) import patterns, implemented auto-creating sync_state row in get_sync_state() method to simplify the API, return 0 for duplicate events in log_event() to indicate successful deduplication. All CRUD operations working with proper error handling and logging.

When D2.1 was not completed, D2.2 created full calendar_scheduler.py. The scheduler has placeholder _fetch_calendar_events() method that will need actual Google Calendar integration via Zo's app integration layer. Recall API rate limit is 60 req/min - 15-min sync cadence is well within safety margin. Health-check command exits non-zero on issues for easy monitoring integration.

The existing calendar_scheduler.py used SQLite database instead of JSON state file as specified in brief - had to overwrite to match architecture requirements. Future drops should verify existing implementations before starting work.
